questions: What exactly does 'render incorrectly' look like? Is it: (a) the image appears blank/black, (b) the image is visually distorted or pixelated, (c) the image is clipped wrong (shows wrong portion), (d) the image position jumps or snaps unexpectedly, or (e) something else? A screenshot of the bug would help calibrate the fix.
Does the bug appear only in the preview (Remotion Player in browser), only in exported video, or both? This determines whether it's a DOM/CSS issue or a Remotion rendering pipeline issue.
Is 2256×1692 the actual problematic image size, or is that just an example? What's the largest image size users typically work with?

The idea mentions 'restore original dimensions when dragged back into view' — does that mean you've observed dimensions being modified automatically when an image goes off-screen? Or is this a feature request for a future smart-resize behavior?
success_criteria: Large images (e.g., 2256×1692 on 1920×1080 comp) render correctly in the preview without visual artifacts
Drag/move operations with large images update Remotion live without rendering glitches
Resize operations cap element dimensions at a safe maximum (≤8192px or whatever threshold Step 2 identifies)
Crop operations work correctly on large/clamped images
No regression for normally-sized clips (≤ composition dimensions)
Exported video with large images renders correctly (no artifacts)
Numeric property inputs in ClipPanel enforce safe max dimensions
assumptions: The bug is caused by oversized DOM elements or Remotion rendering limits, not by incorrect crop/position math — Step 1–2 will validate this assumption
A max element dimension of 8192px is safe for both preview and Remotion export — this is half the browser canvas limit (16384px) and provides margin. Will adjust based on Step 2 findings
Position (x, y) should NOT be clamped to within composition bounds — users intentionally position clips partially off-screen. Only dimensions (width, height) need capping
The fix should be defensive at the render layer (VisualClip) and proactive at the UI layer (OverlayEditor, ClipPanel) — belt and suspenders approach
No database or serialization format changes are needed — this is purely a client-side rendering fix
